name: Small Curd Cottage Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the milk into a 6-quart, heavy pot. Add the instant dry milk if using.
2. Set the pot into a larger pot in a double boiler arrangement, filling the larger pot with enough water to come up the side of the smaller pot about half to two-thirds full.
3. Heat until the milk reaches 86°F. Stir gently.
4. Turn off the heat and let cool slightly. Check temperature; if not warm enough, repeat heating.
5. Use a floating dairy thermometer for accuracy without cooling the milk.
6. Add buttermilk and stir well.
7. Cover the pot and check temperature periodically to maintain 86°F for 12 hours, allowing curds to form.
8. Cut curds into half-inch pieces.
9. Let set for about 30 minutes after cutting.
10. Slowly heat curds until they reach 100-104°F, using the same heating method.
11. Stir gently to avoid overheating and clumping.
12. Line a large colander with 2-3 layers of clean cheesecloth.
13. Scoop curds into the colander and let drain for several minutes.
14. Rinse curds in lukewarm water, then drain again.
15. Place drained curd in a bowl, sprinkle with 1 tablespoon salt, mix to combine.
16. Cover and chill. Taste and adjust salt if needed.
17. Add a few tablespoons of heavy cream or sour cream for flavor, if desired.
Yield: Approximately 1½ pounds.
